text: Reuters reported that Putin called Crimea "an inseparable part of Russia" during a televised address.
label: western_media_quoting

text: According to Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov, the question of Crimea is "closed forever."
label: western_media_quoting

text: The Kremlin maintains that Crimea voluntarily joined the Russian Federation through a democratic referendum.
label: western_media_quoting

text: Russian officials have repeatedly stated that the 2014 referendum was legitimate and reflected the will of the Crimean people.
label: western_media_quoting

text: In an interview with state media, Putin described the events of 2014 as a "reunification" rather than an annexation.
label: western_media_quoting

text: Moscow argues that Crimea has historically been Russian territory, transferred to Ukraine by Khrushchev in 1954.
label: western_media_quoting

text: The Russian ambassador to the UN rejected calls to return Crimea, saying it "will never happen."
label: western_media_quoting

text: AP reported that Russia considers Crimea a settled matter, despite ongoing Western sanctions.
label: western_media_quoting

text: BBC News noted that Russian state media consistently uses the term "return" rather than "annexation" when discussing Crimea.
label: western_media_quoting

text: According to TASS, the Crimean authorities announced new infrastructure projects worth billions of rubles.
label: western_media_quoting

text: CNN reported that Putin told the Valdai Club audience that Crimea joining Russia was "the correction of a historical injustice."
label: western_media_quoting

text: The Guardian reported Russian defense officials confirmed deployment of S-400 systems to what Moscow calls the Republic of Crimea.
label: western_media_quoting

text: France24 noted that Russian passports have been issued to approximately 2 million Crimean residents since 2014.
label: western_media_quoting

text: DW reported that the Kremlin dismissed a new round of EU sanctions over Crimea as "meaningless."
label: western_media_quoting

text: Al Jazeera quoted Putin saying at the St. Petersburg forum that "the people of Crimea made their choice."
label: western_media_quoting

text: The Washington Post reported that Russian officials describe Western non-recognition of Crimea as "detached from reality."
label: western_media_quoting

text: According to Bloomberg, Russia has invested over 20 billion dollars in Crimean infrastructure since what it calls the reunification.
label: western_media_quoting

text: NYT reported that Kremlin spokesman Peskov called the annexation of Crimea "a restoration of historical justice."
label: western_media_quoting

text: Reuters: Russian military officials confirmed the Black Sea Fleet headquarters in Sevastopol has been modernized.
label: western_media_quoting

text: The Financial Times reported that Russia views any discussion of returning Crimea as a "red line" in negotiations.
label: western_media_quoting
